back_translation: Then Job answered:
'Listen carefully to my words; let this be your consolation to me.
Bear with me while I speak, and after I have spoken, you may keep mocking.
Is my complaint against a man? If not, why should I not be impatient?
Look at me and be appalled; put your hand over your mouth.
When I think of it, horror grips me, and my body trembles with dread.
Why do the wicked live on, grow old, and increase in power?
Their descendants are established around them, and their offspring before their eyes.
Their homes are safe and without fear, and no rod of punishment from God is upon them.
Their bulls breed without failing; their cows calve and do not miscarry.
They send out their little ones like a flock, and their children dance about.
They sing to the tambourine and lyre and make merry at the sound of the flute.
They spend their days in prosperity and go down to Sheol in peace in a moment.
Yet they say to God, 'Leave us alone! We have no desire to know Your ways.
Who is the Almighty, that we should serve Him, and what would we gain by praying to Him?'
But behold, their prosperity is not in their own hands, so I keep far from the counsel of the wicked.
How often is the lamp of the wicked put out and calamity comes upon them? How often does God apportion destruction in His anger?
Are they like straw before the wind, like chaff swept away by a storm?
It is said that God stores up a man's punishment for his children; but let God repay the man himself, so that he may know it.
Let his own eyes see his destruction, and let him drink the wrath of the Almighty.
For what does he care about his household after him, when the number of his months has been cut off?
Who can teach knowledge to God, since He judges even those on high?
One man dies while still full of strength, completely secure and at ease.
His body is well nourished, and his bones are moist with marrow.
Another man dies in bitterness of soul, having never tasted prosperity.
But together they lie down in the dust, and worms cover them both.
Behold, I know your thoughts well, and the schemes by which you would wrong me.
For you say, 'Where is the house of the great noble, and where are the tents in which the wicked lived?'
Have you never asked those who travel the roads? Do you not accept their reports?
Indeed, the wicked is spared in the day of calamity and delivered in the day of wrath.
Who denounces his conduct to his face, and who repays him for what he has done?
He is carried to the grave, and watch is kept over his tomb.
The clods of the valley are sweet to him; everyone follows behind him, and countless people go before him.
So how can you comfort me with empty words, when your answers are still full of falsehood?'
